item.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family
item.description: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.

item.title: AFL needs to do more to encourage Indigenous players as marked decline continues at top level
item.description: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

item.title: Volunteer gives central Queensland hospice residents free haircuts
item.description: Discussing how people want to be treated at the end of their life can be hard, but experts say planning can bring dignity and comfort.

item.title: More than 70 UK Labour MPs call for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation — as it happened
item.description: Six ministerial aides have stepped down and more than 70 UK Labour MPs have publicly called for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation.

item.title: Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience to be used to quarantine passengers from hantavirus ship
item.description: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

item.title: Coach Michael Voss resigns from AFL strugglers Carlton, Mick Malthouse says premiership winning player will be relieved
item.description: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.

item.title: Captain of Hantavirus-hit cruise ship praises crew and guests
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship's captain praises crew and guests
item.description: The MV Hondius's captain has praised the patience and kindness of his crew and guests as they disembark.

item.title: Darwin chemical recycler prepares to make diesel from gas condensate
item.description: Global Resource Recovery NT says production of the first diesel made from Central Australian gas condensate will leave Darwin in July.

item.title: Jessica Pegula putting family experience in NFL and NHL to use in tennis prize money protest
item.description: Jessica Pegula is putting her family experience of the NFL and NHL to use in tennis players' fight against the French Open for increased prize money.

item.title: Sitting near the Strait of Hormuz, seafarers are in limbo as Iran war drags on
item.description: As a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, low-wage seafarers are in limbo as they witness an unprecedented 21st-century battle.

item.title: Australian government finalising plans to bring hantavirus cruise ship passengers home
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of the deadly hantavirus outbreak are "in good health", as the Australian government remains hopeful it will finalise plans to repatriate them within 48 hours.

item.title: What giving up alcohol taught me about myself
item.description: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.

item.title: Carlton boss says Voss resignation proof club is in 'genuine transition'
item.description: Carlton CEO Graham Wright says the club will embark on a thorough process to determine the successor to Michael Voss.

item.title: Racist attacks leave New Zealand's Indian community feeling 'hounded'
item.description: Indian communities across New Zealand say they are feeling "hounded" after a surge in racist incidents.

item.title: Four ministers quit as UK PM Starmer tells cabinet he is staying put
item.description: The British prime minister has defied calls to resign, telling ministers he will "get on with governing".

item.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi
item.description: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.

item.title: Year 7 boy trapped under train at North Melbourne station had bag snagged by mirror, school says
item.description: The St Aloysius College student remains in a serious condition in hospital after the incident yesterday afternoon.

item.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access
item.description: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

item.title: Holmes à Court family-owned Heytesbury buys 11 live export ships in major deal with Vroon
item.description: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

item.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report with budget day release
item.description: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.

item.title: Queensland government says it will continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice to ditch project
item.description: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".

item.title: Yindjibarndi people win $150m compensation after Andrew Forrest's Fortescue mines built without permission
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

item.title: Coroner reopens Jayden Penno-Tompsett inquest after remains found in Queensland bush
item.description: The New South Wales man's remains were found in the Queensland bush last October, years after an inquest determined his death was not suspicious.

item.title: Pakistan market blast kills at least nine people
item.description: A blast in a market kills at least nine people as Islamabad blames Kabul for a weekend attack that has left 15 police officers dead.

item.title: Izak Rankine says he has bounced back from suspension for homophobic slur
item.description: After being forced to sit out of last year's finals series thanks to a suspension for a homophobic slur, Izak Rankine is confident he has grown.

item.title: Duo swindled $6 million from five victims in fraudulent property investment scam, court hears
item.description: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.

item.title: Father allegedly high on 'nangs' in crash that killed daughters, as scuffle erupts outside court
item.description: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.

item.title: Federal budget 2026: Winners and losers
item.description: In the first full budget since Labor's landslide election victory last year, the government is putting some money back in workers' pockets and increasing taxes on investors. So who wins and who misses out?

item.title: A five-minute guide to what's in the federal budget to help with cost of living
item.description: Fresh tax cuts, an instant $1,000 tax reduction and cheaper medicines: here's what the federal government's latest budget means for Australians.

item.title: Why Chalmers reined in the property tax lurks in federal budget 2026
item.description: One of the more striking elements from the now well-entrenched program of pre-budget leaks is the lack of outrage from the population or even the opposition.

item.title: $250 tax break for millions of workers in federal budget 2026
item.description: Labor will raise taxes on investment properties and some trust funds, giving the revenue back to workers with a new $250 tax break unveiled in the federal budget.

item.title: Frontbenchers resign as more MPs call for Starmer to quit as UK PM
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is striking a defiant tone despite a growing number of his Labour colleagues urging him to step down.

item.title: Travel quiz, May 13: Find your passport, it's time for a whirlwind world trip
item.description: Where was the world's first permanent commercial bungee jumping site set up? And which country is home to the historic city of Timbuktu?

item.title: Labor bets the house on young Australians' desperation to buy their own
item.description: If the reforms help younger Australians believe home ownership is within reach, Labor can expect to collect a political dividend.

item.title: Jayson Gillham on legal battle with Melbourne Symphony Orchestra and his tour Keys to Life
item.description: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

item.title: Blues On Broadbeach celebrates 25 years of music by the sea
item.description: As some festivals crumble, the Gold Coast's mayor says the 25-year-old Blues On Broadbeach event isn't going anywhere.

item.title: Will CGT and negative gearing budget changes make housing cheaper for first home buyers?
item.description: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.

item.title: Australia-UAE defence commitments do not involve offensive action against Iran, says government
item.description: The Albanese government insists Australia is not involved in any "offensive action against Iran" amid reports the UAE, which had been supplied a Wedgetail aircraft, carried out military strikes against the country.

item.title: Native Pacific black ducks at risk in Tasmania due to introduced species
item.description: The crossbreeding of Pacific black ducks with introduced species has devastated the native species in New Zealand — and conservationists say there's a risk that's happening in Tasmania too.

item.title: Victorian emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition
item.description: A Victorian emergency department nurse and her family face deportation before the end of the month due to her six-year-old son's developmental delay.

item.title: Australian-born, London-based MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to oust Britsh PM Keir Starmer
item.description: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.

item.title: High Court hears bid to overturn block on 22-year Mount Pleasant coal mine extension
item.description: The High Court will hear a major climate change case that could affect mining approvals, as the operator of the Mount Pleasant coal mine seeks to overturn a ruling blocking the project's extension.

item.title: Trump labels critical news reports on Iran 'virtual treason' - as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he does not need help from Beijing to end the war with Iran but says he will talk at length with Chinese President Xi Jinping when they hold a summit this week.

item.title: Midnight Oil frontman Peter Garrett on 'closing the circle' to chair Landcare Australia
item.description: The former frontman for the Australian rockers was among those who helped lobby for the formation of Landcare Australia in 1989, so it seems fitting he should circle back to chair the organisation after a lifetime lobbying for the environment.

item.title: Federal budget disappointment for farmers and environmental groups
item.description: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.

item.title: Jannik Sinner equals record with Novak Djokovic for consecutive Masters 1000 match wins
item.description: World number one Jannik Sinner extended his Masters 1000 winning streak to 31 matches, equalling Novak Djokovic's all-time record.

item.title: PM defends tax overhaul as Coalition accuses him of 'lying' — as it happened
item.description: The Coalition accuses the prime minister of "lying" about changes to tax settings as Anthony Albanese defends the overhaul as necessary for younger Australians.

item.title: Commonwealth Bank shares slump 10.4pc in biggest one-day fall on record
item.description: It was the worst day on the stock market for Australia's biggest bank since March 2020, when it dropped about 10 per cent, with sentiment also weighed by a trading update and broader weakness in bank stocks.

item.title: Former Carlton AFL coach Michael Voss says club in better place since he took over
item.description: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

item.title: Georgia rugby players, including former captain, banned over drug test scheme
item.description: Former Georgia captain Merab Sharikadze was one of six players handed lengthy bans from rugby for their part in a scheme involving alleged sample substitution and advance warnings of drug tests.

item.title: Iran war boosts Beijing ahead of Trump-Xi summit in China, experts say
item.description: As US President Donald Trump prepares to meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing, some analysts say the war in Iran has significantly shifted the power dynamics in China's favour.

item.title: Sunrisers Hyderabad bowled out for 86 in IPL loss to Gujarat Titans
item.description: Australian Test cricketers Travis Head and Pat Cummins have been part of a heavy loss in the Indian Premier League.

item.title: MV Hondius passengers quarantine, fight disease as Dutch and UK governments order isolations
item.description: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

item.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?
item.description: It's been 18 years of hosting Gruen for Wil Anderson, but the self-confessed advertising cynic says the topic is more relevant than ever.

item.title: Tax cut calculator: How much could the 2026 federal budget measures save you?
item.description: How much the budget measures could benefit you depends on how much you earn.

item.title: Southampton beats Middlesbrough 2-1 in extra time to reach EFL Championship playoff final
item.description: Southampton has defeated Middlesbrough to advance to the EFL Championship play-off final after a tense and heated two-leg semifinal.

item.title: Political fight brewing over budget tax reform as Coalition vows to fight Labor's changes
item.description: The Coalition vows to do all it can to prevent property tax reform, which the shadow treasurer says will increase rents and make it harder for young people to buy a house.

item.title: Goldfields women's shelter forced to adapt as abusers use tracking technology
item.description: A women's shelter in regional Western Australia has had to adapt to an increase in technology-facilitated violence to keep victims safe.

item.title: Australia announces squad for Women's T20 Cricket World Cup, Darcie Brown left out
item.description: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

item.title: Paratroopers jump to remote community to deliver medical support
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Inquest into 2023 tasering death of 95-year-old Clare Nowland begins
item.description: Three years after the great-grandmother was tasered by a police officer in her aged care home, an inquest has heard from police and ambulance officers who were at the scene.

item.title: New SA Police search for missing Andamooka mother Trisha Graf
item.description: SA Police say searches in the outback town of Andamooka on Wednesday failed to recover any evidence relating to the disappearance of missing local woman Trisha Graf.

item.title: Eurovision semi finals kick off with musical nod to Austria-Australia confusion
item.description: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

item.title: NSW abolishes good character evidence in sentencing perpetrators of sexual offences
item.description: A survivor advocate behind the campaign calling for good character references to be abolished in sentencing perpetrators of sexual offences has described the NSW reform as "an absolute victory".

item.title: Five-minute solar eclipse in WA's north to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere'
item.description: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

item.title: Gippsland man charged with threatening federal politician on social media
item.description: A man will face court after federal police charged him following an investigation into a social media post that allegedly threatened a federal politician.

item.title: Queensland man released on bail after allegedly ramming synagogue gates with car
item.description: The 32-year-old was allegedly high on drugs when he rammed his car into the gates of Brisbane's largest synagogue in February.

item.title: Community mourns as Mornington Peninsula's Sammy the seal believed dead
item.description: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.

item.title: Farrer's Indigenous and migrant communities worried by rise of One Nation
item.description: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

item.title: WA man accused of terrorism offences faces child abuse, extremist material charges
item.description: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.

item.title: Here's how federal budget negative gearing and capital gains tax changes will affect you
item.description: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.

item.title: Criminal who used tractor to escape prison farm near Cairns gets more jail time
item.description: The Queensland man's escape triggered a month-long manhunt that ended when he was found at a remote campsite near a rainforest.

item.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?
item.description: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.

item.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure
item.description: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.

item.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist urged to test for bloodborne viruses
item.description: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

item.title: Sammy the seal believed to be dead
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: High Court sides with TasPorts over 2022 Mersey River tugboats sinking by Goliath
item.description: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.

item.title: Free omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA for pregnant women in a bid to prevent preterm birth
item.description: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

item.title: Bendigo's Hargreaves Mall could reopen to cars under plan to revitalise area
item.description: A Victorian council is proposing to reopen a regional mall to vehicles after 44 years in an attempt to breathe new life into the area.

item.title: Steven May reveals 'mental breakdown' led to abrupt retirement on eve of AFL season
item.description: Steven May opens up on his shock decision to retire from the AFL just two weeks before the season opener for Melbourne.

item.title: Four charts show Labor's budget is not just about the generation gap, but the wealth gap too
item.description: Just like it did in 2019, Labor is making an argument about wealth in Australia when it proposes negative gearing and capital gains tax changes.

item.title: Yindjibarndi urged to consider appeal against native title compensation decision
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

item.title: Blast furnace worries grow as Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' to federal budget
item.description: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.

item.title: Court grants mining giant Bravus leave to sue council for $22 million over unsealed access road
item.description: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.

item.title: US woman Kouri Richins, who published a book on grief after husband's death, to be sentenced for his murder
item.description: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

item.title: Why Ivan Cleary's decision to leave the Panthers is as unprecedented as their success
item.description: Ivan Cleary is doing what no other coach of his stature has ever done in Australian rugby league history, walking away from the NRL with his team at the height of their powers.

item.title: Labor upgrades net migration numbers by 55,000 in federal budget
item.description: The federal budget reveals the government expects more migrants to arrive to Australia than previously thought, even as it faces a campaign from the Coalition and One Nation to slash migration rates.

item.title: Lawyer for notorious paedophile Peter Liddy says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'
item.description: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

item.title: More than 2,000 protesters chant 'stop the chop' to oppose North Adelaide golf project
item.description: More than 2,000 people gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

item.title: Queensland banking on 'unbelievable' Tamika Upton to level State of Origin series
item.description: Queensland needs a win to keep its State of Origin hopes alive and is turning to the inspirational Tamika Upton to provide it.

item.title: What's in the 2026 budget for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people?
item.description: First Nations experts have their say on whether the $1.2 billion for Closing the Gap initiatives announced in this year's budget is likely to hit the mark.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Broken promises or a roadmap to a fairer Australia?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Cannon by Lee Lai becomes first graphic novel to win the Stella Prize
item.description: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

item.title: Anthony Albanese defends broken promises in budget
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Bill Shorten and Christopher Pyne dissect the budget
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Does negative gearing change entrench inequity? PM responds
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Gunshots ring out at Philippine Senate as ICC seeks arrest of former police chief
item.description: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the Senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.

item.title: Donald Trump and Elon Musk arrive in Beijing ahead of Xi Jinping summit
item.description: The ​US and China are expected to agree to forums to facilitate mutual trade and investment.

item.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
item.description: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift that he did not declare.

item.title: Six dead as Russia launches over 800 drones at Ukraine
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy accuses Russia of attempting to overload Ukraine's defences in an hours-long onslaught.

item.title: Princess Kate tours Italy in first overseas visit since cancer recovery
item.description: The royal is visiting Reggio Emilia, the Italian city famed for its approach to early childhood education.

item.title: How Hollywood became a new home for classical music’s grandest ideas
item.description: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

item.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel will bewitch you in Mother Mary
item.description: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

item.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition
item.description: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

item.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul
item.description: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

item.title: 'From Farage to Farrer': Rising populist tide makes Labor bolder
item.description: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

item.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are meeting in China but only one has the upper hand
item.description: Nine years after his last meeting with Xi Jinping in China, Donald Trump is more aggressive and assertive. But so is Beijing. And its president is ready to take the geopolitical and economic lead.

item.title: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor proposes migration cap tied to housing construction
item.description: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but would wait until in government to decide what visa types to slash if elected.

item.title: Agnes Water accommodation provider fined after refusing to allow woman to stay with guide dog
item.description: A Queensland accommodation provider has been the first to be successful convicted under the state's Guide, Hearing and Assistance Dogs (GHAD) Act.

item.title: Trump touches down in Beijing ahead of Xi meeting
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australians 'literally dying' as aged care average wait times surpass 365 days
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

item.title: Tasmanian government unable to say why its MP has legal fees of $15k
item.description: A large sum of public money has been spent on legal fees for politicians. The Tasmanian government says in one case, it's "not legally possible" to explain why.

item.title: King Charles III opens UK parliament as PM Starmer expected to face leadership challenge
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister remains on life support amid mounting speculation he could face a leadership challenge as early as Thursday, local time.

item.title: How a sister's love unraveled the tragic death of Olympic hopeful Ian Walker and unmasked the teacher who abused him
item.description: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

item.title: TV tower shutdown prompts concern about reliance on foreign satellites
item.description: Phone, internet and council-run TV services have been making way for foreign satellite technology in rural Queensland as ground infrastructure ages, forcing councils to shut down services.

item.title: Former Australia Test cricketer Marcus North named England selector
item.description: The appointment of its first Australian selector, its first female coach and a new-look team has made it a landmark day for England's men's cricket team as it prepares to plunge into its post-Ashes Test recovery.

item.title: Mixed reaction from Australia's diaspora communities to federal budget's housing reform
item.description: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.

item.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform
item.description: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

item.title: Sydney Swans move annual Pride Game away from St Kilda fixture after Lance Collard case
item.description: The fallout continues from the Lance Collard AFL tribunal case, with St Kilda cut from the annual Pride Game against Sydney.

item.title: Michael Voss exit closes door on Carlton's era of premiership contention that never was
item.description: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

item.title: Surfside residents fear homes could be lost to sea in next storm
item.description: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

item.title: Australian dollar could be heading much higher
item.description: In good news for travellers, the Australian dollar is now sitting comfortably above a four-year high as investors see the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates and the RBA hiking them.

item.title: New virus to control surging feral rabbit population at least 10 years away
item.description: Feral rabbits are causing damage on farms and in towns, but biological control research has been delayed by funding woes.

item.title: Live: Taylor details plans to cut migration, scrap net zero and reduce income tax
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has detailed the Coalition's plans to cut migration, scrap net zero policies and reduce income tax in the budget reply. Follow live.

item.title: Drug counsellor Erik Fleming sentenced for role in supplying ketamine to Matthew Perry
item.description: The man who delivered Friends star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him says he is haunted by the mistakes he made.

item.title: US Senate confirms Trump's pick Kevin Warsh to lead Federal Reserve
item.description: The US Senate has confirmed Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's nominee to lead the Federal Reserve, bringing new leadership to the world's most powerful central bank at a fraught moment for the global economy.

item.title: Ben Mack lines up for 231km West Macs Monster race to honour late son and Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Running hundreds of kilometres up and down the Larapinta Trail is a gruelling challenge most people will never attempt. But for Ben Mack, completing it would be a dream come true.

item.title: Public memorial for 702 ABC Sydney presenter James Valentine to be held at Town Hall
item.description: Sydney Town Hall will host a memorial for the late radio presenter and musician, with limited spots available for members of the public.

item.title: SA government plans to remove 10-year fracking ban in state's South East
item.description: The South Australian government is planning to lift a ban on fracking in the state's South East amid reports of increasing risks to gas supply in southern parts of the nation.

item.title: Australia launches first America's Cup challenge in 25 years
item.description: Australia launches its first challenge for the America's Cup in a quarter of a century, entering a team for the 38th edition of the historic sailing event in Naples, Italy, next year.

item.title: Greenbushes residents say Talison's free car wash plan misses the mark
item.description: Residents plagued by dust from Australia's oldest and largest lithium mine say a plan for a free car wash by the mine's owner misses the mark.

item.title: UAE denies meeting with Netanyahu, Iran warns against neighbours 'colluding' with Israel - as it happened
item.description: Donald Trump is expected to ask for China's help to resolve the costly and unpopular conflict with Iran, but analysts say he is unlikely to get the support he wants.

item.title: Dennington Dogs forfeit remainder of men's senior Warrnambool District league season
item.description: A country Victorian football team that was beaten by a collective 1,101 points in four matches forfeits the remainder of the senior season.

item.title: Eddie Jones suspended by Japan rugby for verbal abuse on Australian tour
item.description: Eddie Jones has been suspended from coaching Japan during its rugby union Test match against Italy in July after "incidences of verbal abuse" during a recent tour of Australia.

item.title: How will the NT amend child protection laws? Will the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle change?
item.description: Here's what you need to know about the proposed changes to the NT child protection legislation, introduced into parliament on Wednesday.

item.title: SA Police says fire at North Adelaide Golf Course suspicious
item.description: Police are investigating a suspicious fire that destroyed a temporary structure at the course amid a controversial redevelopment. In a separate incident, work was briefly halted when the perimeter fence was breached, and two people were later arrested for alleged trespassing.

item.title: Federal Court dismisses legal action over Murray flood plain restoration
item.description: A community group's argument that a delegate to the federal water minister made a legal error by approving the Nyah Floodplain Restoration Project has been rejected.

item.title: Palaszczuk partner Reza Adib's first court hearing for rape charges
item.description: A court hearing has been held for Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, for the first time since he was charged with rape, deprivation of liberty and sexual assault earlier this month.

item.title: Coalition to bar non-citizens from welfare, NDIS under fresh migration policy
item.description: Non-citizens, including permanent residents, will be barred from accessing welfare under an updated migration crackdown promised by the Coalition.

item.title: Coles found to have misled shoppers on discounted items in bombshell Federal Court case
item.description: Supermarket giant Coles broke consumer law by misleading shoppers on discount prices, a Federal Court judge has found.

item.title: More than 800 northern corroboree frogs released into the wild in southern NSW to bolster numbers
item.description: More than 800 tiny, technicoloured northern corroboree frogs are released into the wild to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

item.title: Scottish Premier League title race goes to final day with Hearts leading Celtic by one point
item.description: Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho scores a controversial penalty with the last kick of the game to secure a 3-2 win at Motherwell, sending the Scottish Premiership title race to a final-day showdown against leaders Heart of Midlothian.

item.title: Donald Trump tells Xi Jinping they will have 'fantastic future' in bilateral meeting — as it happened
item.description: The highly anticipated meeting saw Donald Trump meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing for the first time in nearly nine years. Take a look back at the historic visit.

item.title: How to clean your sneakers in a washing machine
item.description: Experts reveal their tips on bringing your favourite sneakers back to life at home.

item.title: Newcastle Waters cattle station granted permit to cull 20,000 native birds
item.description: A permit to cull 20,000 native birds has been granted to a cattle station in the Northern Territory in response to an "overpopulation" of destructive and "exceptionally noisy" galahs and little corellas.

item.title: ISIS brides' arrest in Australia prompts anger in Syrian camp
item.description: The arrest of three Australian women linked to the Islamic State group sparks anger and frustration inside the Syrian refugee camp where they had lived for years.

item.title: Xi greets Trump in lavish welcoming ceremony in Beijing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Coles has been found to have misled shoppers. These charts show how
item.description: The pricing patterns of these six products show how Coles was found to have misled consumers through its Down Down pricing patterns.

item.title: Trump hails Xi as ‘great leader’ during Beijing talks
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tasmanian government breaks election promise over TasInsure as 'insurance company' overhauled
item.description: The Tasmanian government will break an election promise to start a state-owned insurance agency, instead establishing a new body to "oversee and support the insurance ecosystem".

item.title: Australian universities may be forced to adopt a definition of antisemitism to address 'sectoral failure'
item.description: The special envoy on antisemitism has found not a single Australian university has met its requirements for adopting a definition of antisemitism.

item.title: Why Gold Coast's retro Chargers jersey is already the toast of the NRL's Magic Round
item.description: One of the best parts of Magic Round is going jersey-spotting but before a ball is kicked in Brisbane the Titans have already cleaned up via their Chargers-inspired retro strip.

item.title: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep
item.description: The Coalition has made a bold play to win back voters with a plan to automatically index income tax brackets to keep up with inflation.

item.title: Police appeal for the driver to come forward following Sunshine Coast crash
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says Mark Opie will continue duties despite AFL probe
item.description: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says team manager Mark Opie will continue his match-day duties despite being investigated by the AFL integrity unit over his links to a bookmaking firm.

item.title: Six passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship due to arrive in Perth tomorrow morning
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak are testing negative for the illness and are symptom-free, the federal health minister says, as they prepare to be repatriated to Australia.

item.title: Rockhampton Regional Council sells six properties at unpaid rates auction
item.description: About 40 people attended the event, including buyer Nathan Casey, who snatched up a three-bedroom home for $200,000.

item.title: 'Pre-existing dispute' likely reason for Wynyard shooting, Tasmania police say
item.description: Police say a shooting yesterday involved people "known to police" and that it "stems from a pre-existing dispute between those two individuals".

item.title: Email emerges showing Jewish group warned police about Bondi gunman, royal commission considers antisemitism definition
item.description: A Jewish security agency raised concerns about Bondi Beach gunman Naveed Akram years before the terror attack that left 15 people dead, a document shows.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick speaks to media in Darwin
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NDIS bill creates funding cut powers, civil penalties and stricter test for access
item.description: Legislation designed to save $35 billion in NDIS spending is introduced to parliament, revealing new powers for the minister to cut funding to classes of supports, automate some decisions and create requirements for prospective participants to exhaust all other treatment options before entering the NDIS.

item.title: Tammy Tyrrell speaks after joining Labor
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Inquest to be held into disappearance of Queensland man Nathan Brosnan
item.description: Nathan Brosnan, 35, went missing from Logan while on parole. He is suspected to have died, but his body has never been found.

item.title: Queensland Minister Amanda Camm denies wrongdoing after shares disclosure
item.description: Under-fire Child Safety Minister Amanda Camm has defended updating her parliamentary register of interests, labelling opposition attacks as "gutter politics".

item.title: Homicide squad investigating suspicious death in Katanning
item.description: The man was found unresponsive on a suburban street in Katanning early yesterday morning.

item.title: Woman dead, man arrested after stabbing in Canberra's inner north
item.description: Paramedics treated the woman for critical injuries at the scene but she was declared dead on her way to hospital.

item.title: NSW Ambulance experts quizzed at Clare Nowland taser-death inquest
item.description: A senior NSW Ambulance officer tells the Clare Nowland inquest that emergency services could have taken more time responding to the 95-year-old's dementia-related aggression.

item.title: Federal MP Joanne Ryan joins calls for Wyndham Mayor Preet Singh to quit
item.description: The mayor of Wyndham City Council, Preet Singh, has faced protests and calls to quit after he wrote a character reference for a man convicted of child sex offences.

item.title: Korean researcher flown out of Antarctica after knife incident at Jang Bogo Station
item.description: A member of an Antarctic expedition who allegedly threatened colleagues with a bladed weapon has been removed from a Korean research station in an emergency evacuation.

item.title: Worst-ever mouse plague in Morawa leaves streets covered in mice
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Roads of dead mice as plague blights standards of living in WA towns
item.description: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.

item.title: 3D-printed guns, taser and ammunition among weapons cache seized in Canberra
item.description: A police investigation has uncovered a large cache of weapons in the national capital's suburbs, less than a kilometre from the Belconnen Police Station.

item.title: Ronda Rousey back for redemption as she fights Gina Carano in MMA bout
item.description: UFC Hall of Famer Ronda Rousey is returning to the ring after almost a decade away, but this time she will be fighting on her own terms.

item.title: Police mine shaft search fails to find evidence of missing SA woman Trisha Graf
item.description: Major Crime investigators have packed up after focusing on outback SA mine shafts in their search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf, who went missing last year.

item.title: Madonna, Shakira and BTS to co-headline World Cup final half-time show
item.description: The first-ever FIFA World Cup final half-time show has been announced.

item.title: How ex-Lambie senator Tammy Tyrell found a home with Labor
item.description: She won office running under Jacqui Lambie's banner, but brand-new Labor recruit Tasmanian senator Tammy Tyrrell says she wanted a "seat at the powerful table of the government".

item.title: Albanese pulls Tammy Tyrrell trick, battlelines drawn in tax fight
item.description: After failing to catch anyone by surprise with the budget, Anthony Albanese had another trick up his sleeve.

item.title: Welcome ceremony for US President Trump's visit to China
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping's meeting in China featured tech titans, Taiwan and flattery
item.description: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.

item.title: Trump asked about talks with Xi Jinping, Taiwan at Temple of Heaven
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Who is in President Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?
item.description: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.

item.title: One dead and many injured after bus rollover on Queensland's Bruce Highway near Gumlu
item.description: Two other passengers suffered life-threatening injuries after the large coach overturned between Townsville and Bowen.

item.title: Illawarra grandmother blames horrific roadside stabbing on Ozempic
item.description: An Illawarra woman stabbed multiple times by her mother-in-law has told a court she continues to be haunted by images of the roadside attack.

item.title: Bosisto's to triple its eucalyptus oil production with new $10 million distillery in central Victoria
item.description: A 174-year-old Australian company is trying to address a decline in local eucalyptus oil production by opening a new distillery in central Victoria.

item.title: Coalition wants to slash migration, but businesses say economy needs skills
item.description: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but businesses say the economy needs a "sustainable" migration program.

item.title: One Nation wedged, Greens steamrolled in SA Labor's two-front environmental battle
item.description: The Malinauskas government's push to end the fracking moratorium in South East SA is a brutal welcome to politics for a new One Nation MP, as Labor fights an environmental war against both the left and right.

item.title: Andrew Forrest's $70m plan to build Exmouth resort gets green light
item.description: After almost a decade of planning, mining billionaire Andrew Forrest's plan to build an eco-resort on the shores of the Ningaloo Reef has received the go-ahead.

item.title: Calls for more education following the suspected death of Mornington Peninsula's beloved 'Sammy' the seal
item.description: Marine experts are calling for greater education on aquatic life following the death of a seal believed to be known by Mornington Peninsula locals as Sammy.

item.title: Live: Miraculous try-savers hand NSW second straight Origin series win
item.description: For the second straight year, NSW secures the Women's State of Origin shield in Game II, with Jesse Southwell and Teagan Berry coming up with stunning try-savers to get the Blues over the line. Follow live.

item.title: Live: Mannagh's heavenly quarter has Lions on the brink against Cats
item.description: Brisbane and Geelong meet for the first time since the 2025 grand final, as the Lions welcome a Cats side out for revenge to the Gabba.

item.title: Traffic stalls on Bruce Highway near Gumlu after bus rollover
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Vision of bus beside Bruce Highway after rollover that injured dozens
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Angus Taylor on mass migration in budget reply
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Angus Taylor announces Coalition's Tax Back Guarantee in budget reply
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Angus Taylor announces immigration cap based on housing in budget reply
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Angus Taylor says Coalition has 'much work' to do to win confidence from Australians
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Angus Taylor's first interview after delivering his budget reply
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Angus Taylor denies immigrants will 'be forced' to give up anything after budget in reply speech
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has defended his comments aimed at non-citizens as necessary and practical during a heated interview on 7.30.
